factors.summary: The company is in contraction mode, with significant workforce reductions across multiple segments, indicating defensive restructuring.

evidence.text: The Energy segment incurred restructuring charges of $30.8 million related to various programs across the segment focused on workforce reductions and field service consolidations. The Engineered Systems segment recorded $13.3 million of restructuring charges relating to headcount reductions across various businesses primarily related to optimization of administrative functions within the Printing & Identification platform and U.S. manufacturing consolidation within the Industrials platform. The Fluids segment recorded $4.9 million of restructuring charges principally related to reduction in workforce for those businesses serving the Pumps markets. Additional restructuring was completed in the pumps businesses for facility consolidation. The Refrigeration & Food Equipment segment recorded restructuring charges of $5.8 million, primarily related to asset impairments due to exit plans at targeted facilities and headcount reductions.

factors.summary: Volume trends are negatively impacted by multiple factors, including declining drilling and production activity in the oil and gas industry, reduced orders from a major food retailer, unfavorable foreign currency translation, and lower sales volumes across most segments. Revenue generated by our Energy segment decreased $533.6 million, or 26.4%, as compared with 2014, composed of an organic revenue decline of 34.3% and an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 1.4%, offset by 9.3% acquisition-related growth. factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs have been implemented, delivering measurable savings in recent years. However, there is an increase in restructuring charges in 2015 compared to 2014, indicating ongoing efforts to manage costs amidst challenging market conditions. The expected savings for 2016 are projected to be between $40 to $50 million, with benefits extending into 2017.

evidence.text: Restructuring programs delivered measurable savings, contributing to margin improvements despite challenges in certain end markets.
The earnings and margin decreases were driven by non-recurring restructuring of $24.9 million... benefits of restructuring programs and productivity initiatives.

During 2015, we continued our focus on operating efficiencies through our Dover Excellence Program. This program... generated approximately $40 million in cost savings during 2015. During the year, we also took actions to right-size our businesses to reflect difficult market conditions, especially within our Energy segment. These actions resulted in full year 2015 restructuring charges of $55.2 million, of which $30.8 million was within the Energy segment. The 2016 cost savings expected to be realized as a result of the restructuring programs and other cost management actions initiated in 2015 is expected to be within the range of $40.0 million to $50.0 million.

We anticipate that much of the benefit of these 2015 programs will be realized in 2016 and into 2017. We also expect to fund the remainder of the 2015 programs currently underway over the next 12 to 18 months. In light of the economic uncertainty in certain of our end markets and our continued focus on improving our operating efficiency, it is possible that additional programs may be implemented throughout the remainder of 2016.
